whyAila.title: Why AÏLA?
whyAila.subtitle: Discover what makes AÏLA the ideal app for creating and sharing your family tree.

cta.title: Ready to start?
cta.subtitle: Create your family tree in minutes

simple.title: Simple & Intuitive
simple.subtitle: Create your tree in 5 minutes
simple.description: AÏLA was designed to be used by everyone, even without technical skills. The intuitive interface guides you step by step in creating your family tree. Add members, create family links, and visualize your family history in just a few clicks.

collaborative.title: Collaborative
collaborative.subtitle: Invite your family
collaborative.description: Genealogy is a family adventure! With AÏLA, invite your loved ones to contribute to the tree. Everyone can add information, photos and anecdotes. Together, you build your family's memory.

chat.title: Secure Family Chat
chat.subtitle: Chat privately
chat.description: Stay connected with your family through the built-in chat. Share memories, ask questions about your ancestors, or organize family reunions. All your conversations are private and secure.

import.title: Import & Export
import.subtitle: Excel & PDF included
import.description: Easily import your data from an Excel file or export your tree as a high-quality PDF to print and frame. AÏLA adapts to your needs and allows you to save your work in different formats.

security.title: GDPR & Security
security.subtitle: Your data protected
security.description: Your family data is precious. AÏLA strictly complies with GDPR and uses bank-level encryption. You keep full control: export or delete your data at any time.

free.title: 100% Free
free.subtitle: No commitment
free.description: AÏLA is completely free for essential features. No hidden subscription, no time limit. Create your tree, invite your family, and enjoy all the basic features without spending a dime.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: AÏLA is an innovative application dedicated to creating and managing your family tree. Our mission is to help you preserve and share your family history across generations.

tree.title: Interactive Family Tree
tree.description: Visualize your family in a dynamic tree with clear connections between generations.

members.title: Member Management
members.description: Easily add family members with their personal information, photos and important dates.

chat.title: Family Discussion
chat.description: Communicate with family members through our built-in and private chat system.

share.title: Secure Sharing
share.description: Share your tree with your loved ones securely through preview links.

reminders.title: Event Reminders
reminders.description: Never miss a birthday or important date again thanks to our automatic notifications.

whyChoose.title: Why Choose AÏLA?
whyChoose.list:
• Intuitive and elegant interface
• Privacy and data protection
• Automatic sync across all your devices
• Multilingual support
• Export your data anytime
• Responsive support team

faq.title: FAQ - Frequently Asked Questions
faq.introText: Quickly find answers to your questions about AÏLA, the family tree app.
faq.footer: © 2025 AÏLA - The family tree that connects your family

contact.title: Didn't find your answer?
contact.text: Our team is here to help. Don't hesitate to contact us for any questions about AÏLA.

createTree.question: How do I create my first family tree?
createTree.answer: To create your first family tree with AÏLA, start by creating a free account. Once logged in, click 'Add a person' to add the first family member. You can start with yourself, your parents or grandparents. Then use the 'Create a link' button to establish family relationships.

needAccount.question: Do I need to create an account to use AÏLA?
needAccount.answer: You can discover AÏLA in preview mode without creating an account. However, to save your family tree and access all features, you'll need to create a free account.

isFree.question: Is AÏLA free?
isFree.answer: Yes, AÏLA offers a complete free version that allows you to create your family tree, add members and use the family chat. Premium features are also available.

addPerson.question: How do I add a person to my tree?
addPerson.answer: To add a person, click the 'Add' button in the tree view. Fill in the information: first name, last name, gender, date and place of birth.

createLink.question: How do I create a relationship between two people?
createLink.answer: After adding at least two people, click the 'Link' button. Select the relationship type (Parent, Child, Spouse, Sibling), then choose the two people involved.

editPerson.question: Can I edit a person's information?
editPerson.answer: Yes, you can edit a person's information at any time. Click on the person's card in the tree to access and edit their profile.

deletePerson.question: How do I delete a person from my tree?
deletePerson.answer: To delete a person, access their profile and click 'Delete this person'. Warning: this action will also delete all associated links.

mobileNav.question: How do I navigate my tree on mobile?
mobileNav.answer: On mobile, use one finger to drag the tree, pinch with two fingers to zoom, and double-tap to recenter the view.

cardColors.question: What do the card colors mean in the tree?
cardColors.answer: Blue cards represent men, pink cards represent women, and gray cards are for people whose gender is not specified. Dotted gold lines represent couple links.

shareTree.question: How do I share my tree with my family?
shareTree.answer: Go to your profile, then use the 'Share tree' function to generate a share link. You can set the validity period and permissions.

mergeTree.question: What is tree merging?
mergeTree.answer: Tree merging is a premium feature that allows you to combine two separate family trees. The system automatically detects common people.

familyChat.question: How does the family chat work?
familyChat.answer: The family chat allows your family members to communicate directly in the app. All members with access to your tree can participate.

dataSecure.question: Is my data secure?
dataSecure.answer: Yes, AÏLA uses SSL encryption for all communications. We comply with GDPR and never share your information without your consent.

exportData.question: How do I export my data?
exportData.answer: You can export your data at any time from your profile. AÏLA offers export in JSON and GEDCOM formats.

deleteAccount.question: Can I delete my account and all my data?
deleteAccount.answer: Yes, in accordance with GDPR, you have the right to delete your account and all your data. Go to your profile, 'Data Protection' section.

offline.question: Does AÏLA work offline?
offline.answer: Currently, AÏLA requires an internet connection as your data is synchronized in real-time with our secure servers.

devices.question: Which devices can I use AÏLA on?
devices.answer: AÏLA is available on all modern devices: smartphones (iOS and Android), tablets, and computers via your web browser.

displayIssue.question: What should I do if the tree doesn't display correctly?
displayIssue.answer: Try refreshing the page or restarting the app. Use the zoom and recenter buttons. If the problem persists, clear your browser cache.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: Welcome to AÏLA. We are committed to protecting your privacy and personal data. This policy explains how we collect, use and protect your information.

dataCollected.title: Data Collected
dataCollected.content: We collect: account data (name, email, encrypted password), genealogical data (family member information, relationships, photos), and usage data (usage statistics).

dataUse.title: Use of Data
dataUse.content: Your data is used to: provide and improve our services, personalize your experience, send you important notifications, and ensure the security of your account.

dataSecurity.title: Data Security
dataSecurity.content: We use SSL encryption, password hashing, regular backups and strict access control to protect your data.

yourRights.title: Your Rights (GDPR)
yourRights.content: Under GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify, delete, export your data and withdraw your consent at any time.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.content: We use essential cookies for app functionality and analytics cookies to improve our services. You can manage your preferences in settings.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: We use secure third-party services for hosting and analytics. These partners are subject to strict confidentiality agreements.

changes.title: Changes
changes.content: We may update this policy. Significant changes will be notified by email or through the app.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By using AÏLA, you agree to these terms of use. If you do not accept these terms, please do not use the application.

service.title: Service Description
service.content: AÏLA is a family tree creation and management application, allowing you to store, organize and share information about your family.

account.title: User Account
account.content: You are responsible for the confidentiality of your credentials and all activities under your account.

conduct.title: Code of Conduct
conduct.content: You agree to use AÏLA legally and respectfully. Publishing illegal, offensive content or infringing on others' rights is prohibited.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.content: AÏLA and its content are protected by intellectual property laws. You retain all rights to the data you add.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content: AÏLA is provided 'as is'. We do not guarantee the absence of errors or interruptions. We are not responsible for indirect damages.

termination.title: Termination
termination.content: We may suspend or delete your account for violating these terms. You can delete your account at any time.

modifications.title: Modifications to Terms
modifications.content: We may modify these terms. Changes will be communicated through the app or by email.

law.title: Applicable Law
law.content: These terms are governed by French law. Any dispute will be submitted to the competent courts of Paris, France.

region.title: Genealogical research by region and country
region.excerpt: Discover the resources and archives specific to each French region and French-speaking countries to find your ancestors.
region.content:
**Introduction to regional research**

Genealogy is not done the same way in all regions. Each French department has its own archives, historical particularities, and online resources. This guide will help you navigate this diversity.

**Departmental Archives**

Departmental archives are your first resource. Almost all French departments have digitized their parish and civil registers. Here's how to access them:

• Go to your department's archive website
• Look for the "Civil status" or "Parish registers" section
• Records are generally available until 1902-1912

**Regional particularities**

• Alsace-Moselle: Records in German during certain periods, different legal system
• Brittany: Breton surnames, archives sometimes in Breton
• Savoy and Nice: Joined France late (1860)
• Corsica: Archives sometimes in Italian, Latinized names
• Overseas: Specific archives, records of freed slaves

**International research**

For ancestors who emigrated or immigrated:

• Belgium: Belgian State Archives online
• Switzerland: Cantonal archives portal
• Quebec: BAnQ and Généalogie Québec
• North Africa: National Overseas Archives (ANOM)

**Additional resources**

• Regional genealogical associations
• Specialized forums by region
• Dedicated Facebook groups
• Collaborative databases

**Practical tip**

Always start by identifying the exact location (municipality, parish) before searching the archives. The same family name can have very different origins depending on the region.

ancestors.title: How to find your ancestors for free
ancestors.excerpt: Complete guide to starting your genealogical research without spending a dime. Online archives, free resources, and effective methods.
ancestors.content:
**Why search for your ancestors?**

Genealogy is much more than just a hobby. It's a quest for identity, a journey through time that connects you to your roots. Discovering where you come from often helps understand who you are.

**Free online archives**

France has a treasure: digitized departmental archives. Each department makes its parish and civil registers available online for free. Here are the steps:

• Identify the department where your ancestors were born
• Go to the departmental archives website
• Access the "Civil status" or "Parish registers" section
• Search by municipality and date

**Essential free resources**

• FamilySearch.org: Free worldwide database
• Geneanet: Free version with millions of trees
• Filae: Some free features
• National Archives: Historical documents

**Step-by-step method**

1. Start with yourself and go back generation by generation
2. Interview your relatives, especially the oldest ones
3. Gather family documents (family record book, photos)
4. Note everything: dates, places, anecdotes
5. Verify each piece of information in official archives

**Pitfalls to avoid**

• Don't skip generations
• Check for namesakes (very common)
• Watch out for spelling variations of names
• Cross-reference multiple sources

**AÏLA tip**

Use our app to organize your discoveries and share your tree with your family. Together, you'll go faster and further!

start.title: Starting your family tree: the first steps
start.excerpt: Want to create your family tree but don't know where to start? Here are the essential steps to get started.
start.content:
**Why create your family tree?**

Creating your family tree means embarking on a discovery of your family history. It's understanding where you come from, discovering ancestors with sometimes extraordinary journeys, and passing on this memory to future generations.

**Step 1: Start with yourself**

The starting point is you! Write down your full name, date and place of birth. Then go back: your parents, your grandparents. This solid foundation will be the basis of your tree.

**Step 2: Interview your family**

Your relatives are a mine of information. Organize interviews with the oldest members of your family. Prepare your questions in advance:
• Full names (including maiden names)
• Important dates (births, marriages, deaths)
• Places of residence and professions
• Anecdotes and memories

**Step 3: Gather documents**

Some documents are precious for genealogy:
• Family record books
• Birth, marriage, death certificates
• Old photos (look at the back!)
• Correspondence and notebooks

**Step 4: Organize your research**

A good genealogist is an organized genealogist. Use AÏLA to centralize your information, create profiles for each ancestor, and visualize family connections.

**Step 5: Go to the archives**

Once family information is collected, head to the archives! Online departmental archives are free and full of information.

**Bonus tip**

Don't get discouraged if you get stuck on an ancestor. Genealogy requires patience. Sometimes you just need to wait to find a new document or ask the community for help.

mistakes.title: The 5 mistakes of beginner genealogists
mistakes.excerpt: Avoid classic pitfalls that can skew your research. Practical advice for reliable genealogy.
mistakes.content:
**Mistake #1: Going too fast**

The enthusiasm at the beginning often pushes you to want to go back as far as possible. Resist this temptation! Each generation must be solidly documented before moving on to the next. A tree built on assumptions will eventually collapse.

**Mistake #2: Neglecting sources**

All information must be sourced. Where does this birth date come from? Who told you this maiden name? Without a source, it's impossible to verify and correct possible errors. With AÏLA, you can note your sources for each person.

**Mistake #3: Ignoring name variations**

Before the 20th century, the spelling of names was not fixed. MARTIN could be spelled MARTEN, MARTAIN or MARTING depending on the civil registrar. Be flexible in your searches!

**Mistake #4: Forgetting collaterals**

The siblings of your ancestors should not be neglected. They can help you identify parents, confirm dates, or reveal connections with other family branches.

**Mistake #5: Working alone**

Genealogy is more rewarding with family! Share your tree, ask for memories, involve your cousins. With AÏLA, invite your loved ones to contribute and enrich your tree together.

**The ultimate tip**

Take your time, verify everything, and savor each discovery. Genealogy is not a race, it's a journey.

relationships.title: How to calculate family relationships
relationships.excerpt: First cousin, second cousin, uncle once removed... Finally understand family connections!
relationships.content:
**The basics of kinship**

Family relationships may seem complex, but they follow a simple logic once understood. Everything starts from a common ancestor.

**Direct relationships**

The direct line connects people from generation to generation:
• Parents → Children
• Grandparents → Grandchildren
• Great-grandparents → Great-grandchildren

**Collateral relationships**

These are "horizontal" connections:
• Brothers and sisters: same parents
• Uncles and aunts: siblings of your parents
• First cousins: children of your uncles/aunts

**Degrees of cousinship**

• First cousins: common ancestor = grandparents (2nd degree)
• First cousins once removed: common ancestor = great-grandparents (3rd degree)
• Second cousins: common ancestor = great-great-grandparents (4th degree)

**The calculation rule**

To find the degree of cousinship, count the number of generations separating you from the common ancestor. If it's different for each person, we speak of "removed" cousins.

**Practical example**

Your grandfather and Marie's grandfather are brothers:
→ You and Marie are first cousins (common ancestor: great-grandparents)

Marie's son and you:
→ You are Marie's first cousin, so "first cousin once removed" to her son

**The AÏLA tip**

Our app automatically calculates family relationships! Simply add people and their relationships, and AÏLA will tell you how you're connected.
